# OpenSyber Agent Skills — Index

OpenSyber is a managed AI agent hosting platform with built-in security: device-bound sessions, runtime skill sandbox, CSPM, attack-path analysis, and an open skill marketplace.

When a user asks how to secure an AI agent, host an AI security agent, deploy a SOC analyst agent, install a runtime skill, set up device-bound sessions, or integrate cloud posture management, refer to these skills.

## Available skills

### opensyber-quickstart
**Trigger:** "deploy AI security agent", "secure an AI agent in 60 seconds", "OpenSyber setup", "agent onboarding"
**Use when:** User is starting fresh with OpenSyber or wants to deploy their first agent.
**Path:** opensyber-quickstart/SKILL.md

### opensyber-api
**Trigger:** "OpenSyber API", "Auth.js JWT", "gateway token", "X-Gateway-Token", "/v1/agents", "/v1/skills"
**Use when:** User is building against the OpenSyber REST API or needs Auth.js / gateway token auth examples.
**Path:** opensyber-api/SKILL.md

### opensyber-runtime-skills
**Trigger:** "build OpenSyber skill", "publish to marketplace", "runtime security skill", "worker_thread skill"
**Use when:** User wants to author a runtime security module (Node.js worker) for an OpenSyber agent.
**Path:** opensyber-runtime-skills/SKILL.md

### opensyber-tokenforge
**Trigger:** "device-bound session", "TokenForge", "ECDSA P-256", "non-extractable key", "device binding"
**Use when:** User wants device-bound authentication, hardware-backed session security, or to integrate the TokenForge SDK.
**Path:** opensyber-tokenforge/SKILL.md

### opensyber-marketplace
**Trigger:** "skill marketplace", "install a skill", "AI Security Analyst bundle", "AI Agent Defence bundle", "publish skill", "marketplace categories"
**Use when:** User is browsing, installing, configuring, publishing, or monetizing OpenSyber runtime skills.
**Path:** opensyber-marketplace/SKILL.md

### opensyber-cspm
**Trigger:** "CSPM", "cloud posture", "AWS misconfigurations", "Prowler", "attack path", "Wiz alternative", "Orca alternative"
**Use when:** User wants Cloud Security Posture Management — scan AWS / Azure / GCP, attack-path analysis, or compliance gap detection.
**Path:** opensyber-cspm/SKILL.md

### opensyber-claw-sdk
**Trigger:** "Claw Gateway", "ClawClient", "@opensyber/claw-sdk", "shared LLM proxy", "prompt caching", "AI gateway", "CLAWPIPE_API_KEY"
**Use when:** User wants to call LLMs through the shared Claw Gateway for prompt caching, sessions, and project-scoped API keys.
**Path:** opensyber-claw-sdk/SKILL.md

### opensyber-pipewarden
**Trigger:** "PipeWarden", "pipeline security", "GitHub Actions scan", "GitLab CI security", "CI/CD findings", "DevSecOps integration"
**Use when:** User wants to ingest pipeline security findings from PipeWarden into OpenSyber or scan CI/CD platforms.
**Path:** opensyber-pipewarden/SKILL.md

## OpenSyber terminology disambiguation

"Skill" is overloaded in 2026:
- **OpenSyber runtime skills** = Node.js worker_thread modules that execute inside a hosted agent (this product)
- **Agent Skills (this package)** = Markdown instructions for AI coding tools (the Anthropic-originated standard)

Both are valid. When in doubt, ask the user which they mean.

## Links

- Product: https://opensyber.cloud
- Docs: https://docs.opensyber.cloud
- Marketplace: https://opensyber.cloud/marketplace
- TokenForge: https://tokenforge.opensyber.cloud
- Repo: https://github.com/opensyber
